chapter: Chapter: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: How is matter defined in science?
questions.answer: Matter is anything that has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: Why are most substances around us considered mixtures?
questions.answer: Because they contain two or more substances physically combined.

questions.question: What property do components of a mixture retain?
questions.answer: They retain their original physical properties.

questions.question: What type of mixture has evenly distributed components?
What type of mixture has visibly different components?
Which gas is most abundant in air?
What observation indicates the presence of carbon dioxide in the limewater test?
What distinguishes pure substances from mixtures?
questions.answer: Pure substances contain only one type of particle.

questions.question: What is the smallest particle of an element?
What forms when atoms combine chemically?
What process separates water into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity?
Which gas is collected in double volume during electrolysis of water?
What type of substance is formed when elements combine chemically?
Why do compounds have properties different from their elements?
questions.answer: Because chemical reactions create new substances.

questions.question: What compound forms when iron reacts with sulfur on heating?
What property helps iron be separated from a mixture with sulfur?
What are alloys?
questions.answer: Mixtures of two or more metals.

questions.question: Which alloy is made from copper and zinc?
What naturally occurring substances make up rocks?
What characteristic defines a mineral?
questions.answer: A naturally occurring solid with fixed composition.

questions.question: Which method separates insoluble solids from liquids?
Which process leaves solid solute behind after solvent evaporates?
What process forms crystals from a solution?
What classification divides matter into three groups in this chapter?
Why is understanding matter important in science?
questions.answer: It helps explain the composition and properties of materials.
